channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia-Ukraine Exchange 205 POWs Each as Kyiv Mourns 24 Dead in Apartment Strike
item.description: Russia and Ukraine carried out their largest prisoner swap of the war, exchanging 205 prisoners each, while rescue workers ended the search for victims of a Russian missile strike on a Kyiv apartment building that killed 24 people including three teenagers.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan Economy Expected to Grow for Second Quarter Despite Iran War Shadow
item.description: Japan's GDP likely rose in January-March on recovering exports and solid domestic demand, a Reuters poll showed, though the full impact of the Iran conflict on the global economy has yet to be felt.

item.title: 🛢️ Gazprom Astrakhan Gas Plant Halts Fuel Output After Drone Attack Fire
item.description: Gazprom's gas processing plant in Russia's southern Astrakhan region has halted motor fuel production after a fire broke out following a drone attack on May 13, two industry sources told Reuters.

item.title: 🇮🇶 Iraqi Parliament Approves Partial Government Under New PM Zaidi
item.description: Iraq's new Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi was sworn in with only a partial cabinet after lawmakers failed to reach consensus on key postings including interior and defense, amid political uncertainty in a country heavily affected by the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇧🇴 Bolivian Miners Clash With Police in La Paz, Demand President Resign
item.description: Violent clashes erupted in La Paz as mining groups took to the streets calling for the resignation of centrist President Rodrigo Paz, with explosions heard during the protests in Bolivia's capital.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Trump Reaffirms Ironclad US-Japan Alliance After China Summit
item.description: Japanese PM Takaichi confirmed that Trump reaffirmed the ironclad bilateral alliance in a phone call after his Beijing visit, signaling continued US commitment to Asian allies despite Trump-Xi talks.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Trump Says Patience With Iran Running Out
item.description: President Trump stated he would not be "much more patient" with Iran, urging Tehran to reach a deal while suggesting the quest for enriched uranium was more about perception than security.

item.title: ⛽ Ukraine Doubles Strikes on Russian Oil Refineries
item.description: Ukraine has stepped up drone attacks on Russian energy infrastructure, doubling the number of oil refineries targeted since the start of the year, according to Russian officials on social media.

item.title: 🇮🇳 India Trade Deficit Widens as Mideast War Drives Oil Imports
item.description: India merchandise trade deficit expanded to $28.38 billion in April as crude imports surged to a six-month high, with Middle East conflict disrupting supplies and raising oil prices.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Trump Discusses Lifting Sanctions on Chinese Companies Buying Iranian Oil
item.description: President Trump said he discussed lifting sanctions on Chinese companies that purchase Iranian oil with Xi Jinping and will make a decision in the coming days, signaling potential policy shift.

item.title: 🇮🇱⚔️ Israel Adopts Rubble Doctrine in Southern Lebanon Security Policy
item.description: A profound shift in Israeli military thinking after October 7 has led to a new security doctrine focused on denying enemy positions in southern Lebanon through systematic destruction, as seen in El-Khiam.

item.title: 🇦🇺🛢️ Australia Rules Out LNG Export Controls as Gas Supply Fears Ease
item.description: Australia will not impose LNG export controls for Q3 2026 after industry assurances eased concerns about east coast gas supply, maintaining its position as a key global LNG supplier.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 AI and Semiconductors Loom Over Trump-Xi Summit Despite Trade Focus
item.description: While trade dominated the Trump-Xi talks, artificial intelligence and semiconductor technology remained an undercurrent in the discussions, highlighting the tech competition between the superpowers.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 US Expects Massive Chinese Farm Purchases After Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: The US anticipates China will commit to buy double-digit billions worth of American agricultural goods following the Trump-Xi summit in Beijing. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer announced the expected deal as Trump concluded his two-day visit.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳🇮🇷 Trump and Xi Agree Iran Cannot Have Nuclear Weapons
item.description: President Trump said he agreed with Chinese President Xi Jinping that Iran cannot be allowed to possess nuclear weapons and must reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Trump warned his patience with Iran was running out.

item.title: ✈️📉 Boeing Shares Drop After China Orders Just 200 Jets
item.description: China agreed to purchase 200 Boeing aircraft, far fewer than analysts expected, sending Boeing shares down 4%. The announcement came during Trump’s Beijing visit and disappointed markets hoping for a larger deal.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Trump Leaves China With Few Wins Despite Warm Words for Xi
item.description: President Trump departed Beijing with no major breakthroughs on trade or concrete Chinese help to end the Iran war, despite two days of praising President Xi. The summit emphasized stability but produced limited tangible results.

item.title: 🇺🇦🤝🇷🇺 Russia and Ukraine Exchange 205 POWs Each in Ceasefire-Linked Swap
item.description: Russia and Ukraine exchanged 205 prisoners of war each on Friday, part of an agreement linked to a ceasefire brokered by President Trump. The swap marks one of the largest exchanges since the conflict began.

item.title: 🇺🇦⚔️🇷🇺 Ukrainian Drone Strike Kills Four in Russian City of Ryazan
item.description: Ukrainian drones killed four people including a child in the central Russian city of Ryazan, damaging high-rise apartment buildings and hitting an industrial facility. The strike comes amid intensifying drone warfare between both nations.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Iran Military Capability Dramatically Degraded, US Admiral Says
item.description: US Central Command commander said American strikes have dealt a major blow to Iran's military and defense industry, leaving Tehran with only a small or moderate ability to strike its neighbors.

item.title: 🇮🇶💰 Iraq Seeks IMF Financial Assistance Amid Iran War Fallout
item.description: Iraqi officials have approached the IMF about securing financial assistance as a result of the Middle East conflict. The war has disrupted trade routes and energy supplies, straining Iraq's economy.

item.title: 🌐🇺🇸🇨🇳 IMF Welcomes US-China Dialogue as Positive for World Economy
item.description: The International Monetary Fund welcomed constructive dialogue between Presidents Trump and Xi, saying reduced tensions between the two largest economies benefits global economic stability.

item.title: 🇦🇪🛢️ UAE Accelerates Pipeline to Double Oil Exports Bypassing Hormuz
item.description: The UAE announced it will accelerate construction of a new oil pipeline to double export capacity through Fujairah by 2027, significantly expanding its ability to bypass the Strait of Hormuz amid Iran tensions.

item.title: 🇺🇦⚔️ Zelenskiy Condemns Russia After Kyiv Apartment Strike Kills 24
item.description: President Zelenskiy called for Moscow to be punished after visiting the rubble of a Kyiv apartment building where a Russian missile strike killed 24 people including three children.

item.title: 📉💰 Global Bonds Tumble as Inflation Fears Mount from Iran War Fallout
item.description: Global bond markets ended a bruising week as economic damage from the Iran conflict prompts investors to expect faster interest rate rises and weaker growth.
